concepts: state-change momentum after regime improvement and post-break hold
mean reversion from exhaustion only when structure reaccepts prior value
breakout continuation with delayed entry after retest rather than immediate trigger
management_styles: one-shot entry with partial take-profit and runner
scale-in on improved confirmation, then scale-out into expansion
partial-profit first target with runner managed by structure instead of fixed take-profit only

next_cycle_focus: Keep only the mechanisms that prove moderate screen density and non-negative expectancy in their best-fit lane. If DOGE session impulse hold expansion and ETH inventory-reset reclaim both trade plausibly, refine exits and regime gating before touching trigger grammar. If only TAO failed-breakdown hold survives, focus future TAO work on cleaner structural entry quality rather than management innovation.
rationale: Cycle 82 is a DOGE-centered 4h explore rotation, but now there is actual evidence from cycle 81 rather than just priors. The last 5 cycles are blunt: 15/15 aborts, with 6 too-sparse failures and 9 bad-idea failures. The two strongest anti-patterns are sparse 4h continuation theater and management-heavy experiments on weak triggers. Cycle 81 added a useful nuance: DOGE value-recovery and timeout-managed retest were not catastrophically wrong in concept, but they still failed the tradeability test on count; meanwhile TAO asymmetric continuation had adequate count and still lost badly, which means the trigger itself was bad. This batch therefore pivots toward denser recurring mechanisms: session impulse hold expansion on DOGE transition, inventory-reset reclaim on ETH chop, and a cleaner failed-breakdown hold on TAO to preserve trend-lane research without repeating the failed asymmetric continuation family.
expected_density_notes: All three chosen mechanisms are expected to be moderate-density on 4h if the thesis is right.
Anything projecting below roughly 15 screen-stage trades should be treated as structurally too sparse for this cycle.
DOGE session-control and ETH failed-flush reclaim should trade more often than TAO continuation structures; TAO is included for asymmetry quality, not raw count.
anti_patterns_avoided: single-bar hero entries with no management logic
management trying to rescue a weak mechanism
reusing known-bad continuation triggers on TAO

top_anti_patterns_to_stop: convenience-first lane selection defended only in prose
management-heavy experiments on weak or anti-correlated triggers
top_promising_structures_to_preserve: continuation only where trend contamination clearly supports it and only from cleaner structural entries
likely_failure_modes: negative expectancy continuation despite adequate trade count
management attempting to rescue a dead trigger
lane mismatch between thesis and live regime

family_kill_events.reason: PF/QScore too weak on first full backtest
